issues.body:
The step at ci.yml:1035-1036 runs `wasm-pack test --node crates/sparq-vectors` with no `--test` filter. wasm-pack builds via `cargo test --no-run --target wasm32-unknown-unknown`, which compiles EVERY `[[test]]` target AND the lib with `--cfg test`. Simultaneously this PR moves `sparq-core = { features = ["mmap"] }` from an all-targets dev-dependency to `[target.'cfg(not(target_arch = "wasm32"))'.dev-dependencies]` (Cargo.toml:249-250), so on wasm32 the `mmap` feature is OFF and every `#[cfg(feature="mmap")]` symbol is absent.

Many test targets reference those absent symbols unconditionally. Concretely, tests/staleness_contract.rs is NOT gated for wasm32 (only tests/wasm.rs carries `#![cfg(target_arch="wasm32")]`) yet calls `g_build.save(&graph_dir)` (line 104) and `Graph::open(&graph_dir)` (line 107); both `Graph::save` and `Graph::open` are `#[cfg(feature="mmap")]` in sparq-core (lib.rs:1740, 1879). Compiling that target for wasm32 fails with E0599 / unresolved-path. 8 further ungated integration tests use the same mmap-only file APIs (import.rs, olympics.rs, delta.rs, store.rs, diskann.rs, metadata_sidecar.rs, throughput.rs, spqv_v3_provenance.rs), and numerous src `#[cfg(test)]` modules pull sparq-core mmap APIs + the rayon/sparq-sim dev-deps that would then be built for wasm32-unknown-unknown. Result: the new CI step does not compile — the lane the PR is trying to add is red, so the intended runtime coverage never runs.

By contrast the existing `wasm-pack test --node crates/sparq-solid` (line 1013-1014) works with no `--test` filter only because sparq-solid's native tests still COMPILE to wasm32 (they reference no wasm-cfg'd-out symbols); wasm-pack merely skips running the non-`#[wasm_bindgen_test]` ones. sparq-vectors' tests do reference cfg'd-out symbols, so they break at compile time, not run time.
issues.fix_hint:
Scope the invocation to the single wasm integration target so cargo does not compile the mmap-dependent tests or the lib's `#[cfg(test)]` modules for wasm32:

  - name: Headless wasm tests (sparq-vectors owned-byte readers, wasm-pack test --node)
    run: wasm-pack test --node crates/sparq-vectors --test wasm

`--test wasm` builds only tests/wasm.rs (plus the lib as a normal rlib, no `--cfg test`), which pulls only `wasm-bindgen-test` and compiles cleanly on wasm32. Verify locally with `wasm-pack test --node crates/sparq-vectors --test wasm`. (Gating all ~30 other test files + src cfg(test) modules with `cfg(not(target_arch="wasm32"))` is the alternative but is far more invasive and easy to regress.)
issues.title: New `wasm-pack test --node crates/sparq-vectors` compiles the whole test suite for wasm32, which no longer compiles once the mmap dev-dep is target-gated off wasm32

summary: The new tests/wasm.rs is well-built and non-vacuous: I checked every fixture byte-offset against the real validators (SPQV: magic/ver/dim/count u64 + 12 reserved = HEADER_LEN_V1 32, +24 fingerprint = HEADER_LEN 56, then dim*4 f32 data, then count*8 id->slot index with strictly-ascending ids 7/42/99 and slots 0/1/2; SPQG: magic/ver/dim/degree/count u64/medoid/enc_tag(0)/24-byte fp, then record_len = 8 + dim*4 + degree*4 node records), and the assertions produce exact floats (nearest_exact's dot/(|a||b|) and DiskAnn's 1 - d^2/2 both yield exactly 1.0/0.0/-1.0 for the chosen unit vectors), so the == comparison is not float-flaky. However there is a BLOCKER in the CI wiring. The PR moves the `sparq-core` dev-dep (which supplies the `mmap` feature) to `cfg(not(target_arch="wasm32"))`, so on wasm32 the mmap-gated symbols disappear. But the new step runs `wasm-pack test --node crates/sparq-vectors` with NO `--test` filter, which compiles EVERY test target plus the lib with `--cfg test` for wasm32. Multiple ungated integration tests (e.g. tests/staleness_contract.rs:104/107 call `Graph::save`/`Graph::open`, both `#[cfg(feature="mmap")]` in sparq-core; also import.rs, olympics.rs, delta.rs, store.rs, diskann.rs, metadata_sidecar.rs, throughput.rs, spqv_v3_provenance.rs) reference symbols that no longer exist on wasm32, and many src `#[cfg(test)]` modules do the same. The step will fail to COMPILE on wasm32, so the lane it adds is red. sparq-solid's identical no-filter invocation works only because its native tests still compile to wasm32; sparq-vectors' do not. Fix: scope the command to the wasm target: `wasm-pack test --node crates/sparq-vectors --test wasm`.
